planner.title: Date planner
planner.disconnectConfirm: Disconnect Google Calendar? Existing events will remain but no further changes will sync.
planner.syncCreated: {count, plural, one {# event created} other {# events created}}
planner.syncLinked: {count, plural, one {# manual event linked} other {# manual events linked}}
planner.syncFailed: {count, plural, one {# failed (see console)} other {# failed (see console)}}

client.accessBlockedPaused: The client is paused. They can't access their panel until you change the status to Active or In review.
client.accessBlockedInactive: The client is inactive. Their session is disabled until you reactivate the status.
client.noCredentialsBody: This client doesn't have an email + password yet to sign in to their personal panel.
client.noNotesAdmin: Write the first one to give context to the client.
client.noNotesClient: Write the first one to send a message to the team.
client.noteAdminPlaceholder: Leave an internal note or message for the client...
client.noteClientPlaceholder: Share feedback, questions or ideas with the team...
client.notesHintAdmin: Enter to publish · Shift+Enter for new line · Notes are visible to the client.
client.notesHintClient: Enter to publish · Shift+Enter for new line · The admin team will receive this note.
client.noActiveAds: No active ads for this client
client.leadsPipelineFuture: In the next phase this pipeline is integrated directly here
client.calendarBody: Mini-calendar where stage events for {name} are created. Future changes go to the selected calendar; existing events stay where they are.
client.calendarNone: No calendars found where you can create events in the connected account.
client.driveBody: Drive folder where the documentation for {name} lives. Paste the full URL. The Open button will appear next to every stage marked as Ready in the Planner.
client.accessSectionBody: Credentials with which {name} signs in to their personal panel.
client.dangerZoneBody: Permanently delete this account and all its associated data (notes, API connections, dates, metrics).
client.estadoConfirmBodyBlock: By changing the status of {name} to {newStatus}, the client won't be able to access their personal panel even if they enter their credentials correctly.
client.estadoConfirmBodyRestore: By changing the status of {name} to {newStatus}, the client will regain access to their personal panel.
client.assignBody: Define the email and password the client will use to sign in. Save them and share them through a secure channel.
client.editCredBody: Change the email and/or password. Leave the password blank to keep the current one.
client.deleteConfirmIrreversible: This action cannot be undone. The following will be permanently deleted:
client.deleteConfirmItem1: Client data, social networks and stages

api.tabBody: The connections you authorize here feed the real data of {metrics}, the {report} button for this client, and the global report data in {metricsGlobal} → ROAS. Access is granted via OAuth — the CRM never stores client passwords.
api.howItWorks1: Clicking "Connect with [provider]" opens the provider's official sign-in screen.
api.howItWorks2: The client (or you, with their authorization) accepts the requested permissions.
api.howItWorks3: The provider returns an encrypted access token — we never share or store passwords.
api.howItWorks4: Credentials renew automatically. You can disconnect at any time.
api.metaDesc: Connect Facebook + Instagram for real-time campaign, reach, engagement and followers metrics.
api.googleDesc: Connect Google Ads for search, display campaigns and conversion data.
api.disconnectConfirm: Disconnect {provider}? Metrics will stop updating in real time.
api.scopesIntro: This connection will allow the CRM to:
api.redirectNotice: We'll redirect you to {provider} to authorize access securely.

newClient.title: New client
newClient.subtitle: Fill out the details to create the account and its access
newClient.errorsHeader: {count, plural, one {1 field needs to be completed correctly} other {# fields need to be completed correctly}}
newClient.errorsHelp: Check the fields highlighted in red below.
newClient.errorNameExists: A client with that name already exists
newClient.errorRedesEmpty: Select at least one social network
newClient.step2Warning: Save these credentials and share them with the client through a secure channel.

setup.title: Configure your backend
setup.subtitle: A few steps are still pending. Once they're done, the CRM is production-ready.
setup.stepEnvTodoDesc: Environment variables are missing. Create a Supabase project and paste NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY into your .env.local. Restart the dev server afterwards.
setup.stepMigrationsTodoDesc: The profiles table doesn't exist yet. Open supabase/migrations/*.sql in order and paste each into Supabase's SQL Editor.
setup.stepAdminTodoDesc: No user has the admin role yet. Create one from Authentication → Users → Add user (not via SQL — that breaks auth.identities). Then run the INSERT block in 003_supabase_auth.sql to promote it.
setup.stepAdminDoneDesc: At least one admin is registered.
setup.stepGoogleOptionalDesc: Only needed if you want the Planner to sync events to Google Calendar. Create OAuth credentials and paste GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET.
setup.docsHint: Need more detail? See the full guide in
